title: Procter & Gamble Co. (PG) — Intrinsic Value & Prospect Score
description: Opus 4.6 independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Procter & Gamble Co. (PG). Economic Prospect Score: 66/100 — Moderate Prospect.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y10)
fcfGrowth.rationale: Procter & Gamble Co. is projected to grow FCF at 6% annually, reflecting its competitive position, sector dynamics, and historical compounding rate.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 8.0% WACC balances Procter & Gamble Co.'s earnings quality and balance sheet strength against sector volatility and macroeconomic risk factors.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.5% terminal rate assumes long-run growth converges toward nominal GDP, appropriate for a mature S&P 500 constituent.

thesisTitle: Procter & Gamble Co.: A Fundamental Assessment
thesisParagraphs: Procter & Gamble Co. (PG) is a large-cap constituent of the S&P 500 with established market position and consistent cash flow generation. The company's competitive advantages and market share provide a degree of pricing power and earnings stability that supports a premium valuation.

My DCF applies a 6% FCF growth rate reflecting the company's historical compounding ability and forward outlook, with a 8.0% discount rate capturing sector-specific risks and capital market conditions. The valuation is sensitive to assumptions about sustainable growth — investors should stress-test these inputs against their own convictions.
verdictFooter: Opus 4.6 independent analysis. Data via public filings (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: What is Procter & Gamble Co.'s intrinsic value according to Opus?
faq.answer: Based on a 10-year DCF with 6% FCF growth and 8.0% WACC, Opus calculates PG's intrinsic value at approximately $155.69 per share, suggesting the stock is fairly valued at $170.00.

faq.question: What assumptions drive PG's valuation?
faq.answer: The key drivers are the FCF growth rate (6%) and discount rate (8.0%). A 1% increase in growth adds roughly 10-15% to the intrinsic value. These assumptions reflect Opus's independent assessment of the company's competitive position and risk profile.

faq.question: Should I buy or sell based on this analysis?
faq.answer: This is not financial advice. DCF models are inherently subjective — small changes in assumptions create large swings in output. Use this as one data point among many in your investment research process.

prospectVerdictDetail: P&G is the definition of a quality compounder — dominant brands, pricing power, and consistent execution. But growth is limited to low-single-digits organically, and the stock trades at a premium that assumes flawless execution. The company benefits from trade-down in recession and trade-up in expansion, but transformative growth is structurally capped.
prospectKeyRisks: Volume growth stalling as price increases reach consumer resistance levels and private-label alternatives gain share
Emerging market currency headwinds and geopolitical risks reducing international growth contribution
Innovation pipeline failing to create genuinely new categories — incremental improvements to existing brands have diminishing returns
prospectKeyCatalysts: Premiumization strategy (SK-II, Olay Regenerist) driving mix improvement and margin expansion in personal care
Digital marketing and DTC efficiency gains reducing cost-per-acquisition and improving advertising ROI
Emerging market middle-class growth driving volume expansion for P&G's household and personal care brands
prospectMethodology: Score is based on three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30), totaling 0-100. Each pillar is broken into individually scored factors with transparent rationale. Data sources include FY2025 10-K filings, analyst consensus estimates, news sentiment analysis, and competitive landscape assessment. The score is forward-looking and represents economic prospect over a 2-3 year horizon.
